answer:
**The Yama Smṛti (Yama Smṛti), Chapter 2, prescribes these exact fourfold-value fines for the specified grocery adulterations.**

The first verse covers the ghee adulterated with sesame oil (or vice versa):

> Ghee sold mixed with sesame oil under the name of ghee, / the merchant shall be made to pay four times the value; or oil mixed with ghee. // YSS_2.37 //[^1]

The immediately following verse lists the other adulterations:

> Pepper with stone fragments, salt with earth, / and asafoetida with tree resin—he shall likewise be made to pay four times the value. // YSS_2.38 //[^2]

These appear consecutively amid rules on fines for merchants and sellers (e.g., false weights, grain mixing). Yama Smṛti is a Sanskrit dharmashāstra text concerned with penances, punishments, and social regulations.[^3]

[^1]: Yama Smṛti, [Chapter 2, p-551495](/works/unknown-yama-smrti-552f8d/02/translation/#p-551495).

[^2]: Yama Smṛti, [Chapter 2, p-6039eb](/works/unknown-yama-smrti-552f8d/02/translation/#p-6039eb).

[^3]: Yama Smṛti, [Chapter 2](/works/unknown-yama-smrti-552f8d/02/translation/).

args.query: pepper stone fragments salt earth asafoetida resin ghee sesame oil fourfold
"fourfold" adulterat* pepper OR salt OR asafoetida OR ghee
